page: Amazon Web Services r5n.large is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, meaning storage relies entirely on network-attached EBS volumes.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r5n.large sits in the lower tiers for compute and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core score of 1319.59 and multi-core score of 1733.09 reflect weak overall CPU performance. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show 93.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drop to 13.58 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations under Passmark score at 680.92, while Redis SET operations achieve 1.66 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5n.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for entry-level workloads requiring more RAM than compute power, without the overhead of larger instance sizes. However, the trade-off is limited processing capability and lower baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. This instance is best suited for small-scale memory-intensive applications, lightweight caching layers, development databases, or basic web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over high-throughput CPU execution.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.large is a memory-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1733.09 and a Passmark memory score of 1660.52. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is measured at 13.58 GB/sec. Given its high memory density relative to its processing power, the r5n.large is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU performance. It is suited for lightweight database operations, caching systems, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor, 2 vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for entry-level database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Nitro hypervisor architecture with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight caching, small databases, and memory-bound applications
